name: Almería and the Coast Loop
name_original: Paseo Marítimo de Ribera — circular por Almería
description: Cycle the moderate 23.0-mile Paseo Marítimo de Ribera circular route in Almería for stunning Mediterranean views and coastal scenery.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Campo Nijar Y Bajo Andarax offers diverse landscapes, from the volcanic topography of the Cabo de Gata-Níjar Natural Park to the arid canyons of the Tabernas Desert. The region features rolling hills, coastal plains, and fertile valleys along the Andarax River, providing varied terrain for cyclists. Routes often traverse semi-desert environments, mountain ranges like Sierra Alhamilla, and picturesque white villages. This area is characterized by its unique geological formations and a mix of paved and unpaved trails suitable for touring bicycles. Best touring cycling routes around Campo Nijar Y Bajo Andarax The most popular touring cycling route is Salinas de Cabo de Gata — circular from San José — Cabo de Gata-Níjar Natural Park, a 25.2 miles (40.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 0 minutes to complete. This route explores the coastal salt flats and volcanic landscapes within the natural park. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Transandalus Trail: Agua Amarga Loop, a moderate 11.8 miles (19.0 km) path. This trail offers scenic views of the coastline and passes through the charming village of Agua Amarga. Local touring cyclists also love the Rodalquilar and Caldera de Majada Redonda Loop, a 16.1 miles (25.9 km) trail leading through volcanic caldera landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 9 minutes. Touring cycling around Campo Nijar Y Bajo Andarax is defined by volcanic coastlines, arid desert plains, and green river valleys. The extensive network offers options for various ability levels, from leisurely rides through agricultural areas to more challenging routes in mountainous terrain. The routes in Campo Nijar Y Bajo Andarax are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 900 reviews. More than 8,000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Campo Nijar Y Bajo Andarax's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Campo de Níjar y Bajo Andarax?
A: There are over 300 touring cycling routes available in Campo de Níjar y Bajo Andarax, offering a wide variety of options for different skill levels and preferences.

Q: What are the general difficulty levels of touring cycling routes in the region?
A: The routes in Campo de Níjar y Bajo Andarax cater to various abilities. You'll find approximately 70 easy routes, nearly 100 moderate routes, and around 145 difficult routes, ensuring there's a suitable challenge for every touring cyclist.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Campo de Níjar y Bajo Andarax?
A: Thanks to its favorable climate, with over 3,000 hours of sunshine annually and average temperatures between 16-24°C, Campo de Níjar y Bajo Andarax is ideal for touring cycling year-round. The period between December and April is particularly pleasant as the arid landscapes often blossom with flowers.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Campo de Níjar y Bajo Andarax?
A: Yes, many routes in the region are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. For example, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1039189417/salinas-de-cabo-de-gata-circular-from-san-jose-cabo-de-gata-nijar-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salinas de Cabo de Gata — circular from San José&lt;/a&gt;, which explores coastal salt flats, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1041915344/transandalus-trail-agua-amarga-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Transandalus Trail: Agua Amarga Loop&lt;/a&gt; for scenic coastal views.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while touring cycling in this region?
A: The region offers incredibly diverse landscapes. You can cycle through the unique volcanic topography of the Cabo de Gata-Níjar Natural Park, experience the arid canyons of Europe's only desert, the Tabernas Desert, or enjoy the green citrus groves of the Bajo Andarax Valley. Mountain ranges like Sierra Alhamilla also provide varied terrain and views.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or those looking for an easier ride?
A: Absolutely. With approximately 70 easy routes, beginners can enjoy leisurely rides through less challenging terrain. These often traverse coastal plains or agricultural areas, providing a gentle introduction to the region's beauty. Look for routes with lower elevation gains and shorter distances.

Q: Are there any longer touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Experienced riders will find plenty of challenging, longer routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6882748" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabo de Gata Lighthouse – Collado de la Vela Blanca loop from Centro de visitantes Amoladeras&lt;/a&gt; covers over 55 km, offering significant elevation changes and stunning views. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1055796448/crater-of-la-granadilla-circular-from-nijar-by-the-via-verde-of-lucainena-de-las-torres" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Crater of la Granadilla — circular from Níjar&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 46 km route.

Q: What interesting places can I explore along the touring cycling routes in Campo de Níjar y Bajo Andarax?
A: The region is rich in attractions. You can visit historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2846137" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcazaba of Almería&lt;/a&gt;, explore natural wonders such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/635124" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tabernas Oasis&lt;/a&gt;, or enjoy coastal views from &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3291513" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views from the Granatilla viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/125186" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Genoveses Beach&lt;/a&gt; is also a popular spot for its pristine beauty.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about touring cycling in Campo de Níjar y Bajo Andarax?
A: The komoot community highly rates the touring cycling experience here, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 900 reviews. Cyclists often praise the region's diverse landscapes, from volcanic coastlines to arid desert plains and green river valleys, as well as the extensive network of routes suitable for various ability levels.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through charming villages or historical sites?
A: Yes, many routes will take you through picturesque white villages and past historic cortijos. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1041874843/rodalquilar-and-cortijo-del-fraile-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rodalquilar and Cortijo del Fraile Loop&lt;/a&gt;, for example, offers a chance to see traditional architecture and historical points of interest, including the famous Cortijo del Fraile.

Q: Can I find routes that offer coastal views and access to beaches?
A: Absolutely. The Cabo de Gata-Níjar Natural Park, which forms a significant part of the Níjar coastline, offers numerous routes with stunning sea views and access to secluded coves and pristine beaches. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1042129837/almeria-and-the-coast-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almería and the Coast Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a great option for coastal cycling.

Q: Are there routes that explore the volcanic landscapes of the region?
A: Yes, the region's unique volcanic topography is a highlight for many routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1039226890/rodalquilar-and-caldera-de-majada-redonda-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rodalquilar and Caldera de Majada Redonda Loop&lt;/a&gt; specifically leads through volcanic caldera landscapes, offering a truly distinctive cycling experience.
tips: An ideal place to enjoy the beautiful views of the sea with a bike lane that runs along the entire promenade of the city.

The Cathedral of the Incarnation of Almería is a majestic architectural work that dates from the 16th century. Located in the historic center of the city, it is an example of Spanish Renaissance and Baroque architecture. The cathedral is known for its impressive bell tower and impressive interior, which houses a rich collection of sacred art and religious ornaments. A must for those interested in history, religion and architecture.

The Alcazaba of Almeria is a Moorish fortress that overlooks the entire city of Almeria.
The Alcazaba was built under Caliph Abd ar-Rahman III in the 10th century. built on a 85 m high hill in the center of the city. Since the city was formally founded in 955, it has housed the residence of the city lords. During the Taifa period from 1012 to 1091, when the city was independent, it became the residence of sovereign princes who temporarily claimed the title of caliph. Al-Mutasim in particular made a name for himself as a builder from 1051 to 1091.
The Alcazaba was used by Muslim governors up until the 15th century. In 1522 the palace complex was destroyed by an earthquake and partially replaced by new buildings. In the course of these reconstruction measures, parts of the Alcazaba were shaped by Christian architecture and the mosque was converted into a chapel.

faqs.question: Where can I park and access the Paseo Marítimo de Ribera cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is a circular one along the coast of Almería. You can typically find parking along the Paseo Marítimo de Ribera itself, which serves as a dedicated cycle way. As it's a circular route, you can start at various points along the promenade.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: Given Almería's Mediterranean climate, spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) are generally the best times to cycle this route. The weather is milder, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler, potentially wetter days of winter. You can also enjoy spectacular sunrises or sunsets over the Mediterranean Sea during these periods.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the route primarily follows a dedicated bike path along the promenade, specific regulations for dogs on cycling paths can vary locally. It's always best to check local signage regarding pets, especially if you plan to bring your dog. If allowed, ensure your dog is well-behaved and on a leash for the safety of all users.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees to cycle here?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or have to pay entrance fees to cycle the Paseo Marítimo de Ribera. It is a public promenade and cycle path accessible to everyone.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Paseo Marítimo de Ribera circular route?
faqs.answer: The route is classified as a moderate bike ride. While much of it features paved surfaces, as it follows the Paseo Marítimo de Ribera, some segments could be unpaved and potentially challenging. It requires a good level of fitness due to its length and some elevation gain.

faqs.question: What notable landmarks or viewpoints can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you'll enjoy excellent views of the Mediterranean Sea. You'll pass by the Paseo Marítimo de Ribera itself, and the route is close to significant historical sites like the Almería Cathedral (Cathedral of the Incarnation) and the Alcazaba of Almería. The wider coastal area also offers elevated viewpoints such as the Cabo de Gata Lighthouse and the White Sailing Tower.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as 'moderate' and is approximately 37 km long with about 270 meters of elevation gain. It requires a good level of fitness. While there's a dedicated bike path, some sections might be unpaved. Beginners might find the distance and elevation challenging, so it's best suited for cyclists with some experience.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with other well-known cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route connects to and includes sections of the EuroVelo 8 route, which is part of a larger, recognized cycling network. It also overlaps with parts of the Transandalus and the Camino Mozárabe. Almería-Granada trails.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the route?
faqs.answer: As the route follows the Paseo Marítimo de Ribera in Almería, you will find numerous cafes, restaurants, and shops along the promenade and in the city center where you can stop for refreshments or a meal.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a touring bicycle ride on this trail?
faqs.answer: For a moderate touring bicycle ride of this length, it's advisable to bring plenty of water, snacks, sun protection (sunscreen, hat, sunglasses), and appropriate cycling attire. A basic repair kit and a charged phone are also recommended. Given the coastal views, you might also want to bring a camera!

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this circular cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 37.0 km long and cyclists with a good fitness level can typically complete it in about 2 hours and 36 minutes. However, this duration can vary based on your pace, stops for sightseeing, and fitness level.
